indicator_text: Strickland 2014 applies Newmark's (2005) 18-item lobby-regulation measure without adding, removing, or modifying any items. He decomposes the index into its three components (definitions, prohibitions, reporting requirements) and uses them as three separate explanatory variables with two interaction terms (Definitions x Prohibitions; Definitions x Reporting), but the 18 underlying items are Newmark's verbatim. Strickland's contribution is temporal extension (1988-2013 vs. Newmark's 1991-2003), not rubric construction.
scoring_rule: In this index, lobby laws come in three categories: definitions of lobbyists, prohibitions on their conduct, and reporting requirements. I utilize the three categories of lobby laws in Newmark's index as separate explanatory variables. Table 1 lists all 18 laws included in Newmark's scale.
source_quote: To gain a sense of how lobby laws have changed over time, I employ a measure of lobby law stringency that spans multiple states and years. Newmark's (2005) measure assumes values from 0 to 18 depending on the presence or absence of specific lobby laws... As Newmark's data covered only the years 1991 to 2003, I extended the index to cover the years 1988 to 2013 using a method described in the appendix. papers/text/Strickland_2014__lobbying_laws_interest_groups.txt L416-429
notes: Strickland's Table 1 reproduces Newmark's 18 items verbatim, attributed via "Source. Newmark (2005)." Strickland does NOT construct a new rubric; he applies the existing one. Modifications: (a) decomposes 0-18 sum into 3 sub-scales used separately in regression, (b) adds two interaction terms in regression specification (analytic choice, not item-level change), (c) extends temporal coverage 1988-2013 using new source documents (Council on Governmental Ethics Laws Blue Book and Book of the States pre-2004; State Capital Law Firm Group's Lobbying, PACs, and Campaign Finance: 50 State Handbook post-2004). Per the instruction's "valid output if applied unchanged" guidance, this TSV intentionally has zero own-rubric rows.
